sessions.intent: First codeanalyzer pass over the lazy-deserialize feature (Data lazy materialize, reader registry, binding-clone, wire read/write, goal-call params).

before.assumptions: Coder v3 reports GREEN (272/272 goal, 4021/0 C#). Feature is large (~414 .cs files vs runtime2 base). Highest risk is the recent binding/clone + wire-shape reconstruction commits and the dense variable.set Run().
before.risk: set.cs Run() is ~190 lines with deep branching; lazy materialize + clone semantics + wire-shape sniffing are subtle and could carry root-cause-vs-symptom smells.

actions.context: Five-pass review focused on the lazy-deserialize mechanism + the highest-risk recent commits (binding-clone, wire-shape reconstruction, goal-call param clone). Mechanical bans (System.IO, Console, OBP #9) grepped clean across the whole changed surface.

Rebuilt clean. C# suite 4021/0. Goal suite 271 pass / 1 fail — the single fail is a live httpbin.org 503 (network-flaky, passed at 14485ms earlier same session, failed at 574ms now). Not a regression; flagged R1 to disable in-goal like its 8 siblings.

Findings F1 (list-element raw-vs-Data, OBP #5, blocker), F2 (Materialise/Materialize naming footgun), F3 (duplicated AsT Resolve block), F4 (CanonicaliseKind re-derive symptom), F5 (set.cs Run length), R1 (flaky httpbin test). Verdict NEEDS WORK / FAIL.

after.health: Green suites, no correctness regression. Blocker-level cleanups F1+F2 before PASS.
after.notes: F1 is the strongest: this branch's own shallow-clone fix made list.add store Data while list-creation still stores raw; consumers (List.Element, WrapItem) defensively unwrap. Store uniformly + add the signed-Data-in-collection regression the coder already identified. Next bot: coder.
